article_title: Inside ProMedica’s path back to investment-grade status
reason: Exited the skilled nursing facilities market due to significant operating losses.
member_impact: Removed a major source of financial drag, leading to stabilization.
summary: ProMedica exited the skilled nursing facilities market, which was responsible for over $200 million in operating losses in 2022. This move helped stabilize the system financially. The exit was part of a broader turnaround strategy to focus on core medical care.

article_title: Inside ProMedica’s path back to investment-grade status
reason: Sold its national hospice business to strengthen liquidity.
member_impact: Used proceeds to pay down over $400 million in debt.
summary: ProMedica sold its national hospice business through a competitive bidding process, which allowed the system to pay down more than $400 million in debt and improve liquidity.

article_title: Inside ProMedica’s path back to investment-grade status
reason: Sold its insurance business to simplify operations.
summary: ProMedica sold its insurance business to Medical Mutual of Ohio, which simplified its operating model and allowed the system to focus more on provider operations.

article_title: Inside ProMedica’s path back to investment-grade status
